description: Fix the httpStatusToToolError table in SDK utilities docs (429 retryable value wrong) and the ProgressFlow illustration (wrong IPC mechanism label).
qualityChecks: cd docs && npm run build && npm run type-check && npm run lint && npm run knip && npm run format:check

userStories.title: Fix httpStatusToToolError retryable values in SDK utilities
userStories.description: As a plugin developer, I want the httpStatusToToolError table to show correct retryable values so that I understand which HTTP errors are retryable.
acceptanceCriteria: The httpStatusToToolError table row for 429 shows `true` in the retryable column (not em-dash)
The httpStatusToToolError table row for 401/403 shows `false` in the retryable column (not em-dash)
The httpStatusToToolError table row for 404 shows `false` in the retryable column (not em-dash)
The httpStatusToToolError table row for 'Other' shows `false` in the retryable column (not em-dash)
The lastUpdated frontmatter is set to 2026-02-28
cd docs && npm run build passes
cd docs && npm run type-check passes
cd docs && npm run lint passes
cd docs && npm run knip passes
cd docs && npm run format:check passes
userStories.notes:
File: docs/content/docs/sdk/utilities.mdx

Lines 437-444: The httpStatusToToolError mapping table uses em-dash (—) for the retryable column in several rows. The actual source at platform/plugin-sdk/src/fetch.ts shows:

- 401/403: Uses ToolError.auth() which sets retryable: false
- 404: Uses ToolError.notFound() which sets retryable: false  
- 429: Uses ToolError.rateLimited() which sets retryable: true
- 503: Uses new ToolError(..., { retryable: true }) — already shows `true` in docs, correct
- 5xx (other): Uses new ToolError(..., { retryable: true }) — already shows `true` in docs, correct
- Other: Uses new ToolError(..., { category: 'internal' }) with default retryable: false

Replace all em-dashes in the retryable column with explicit `true` or `false` values. The table is a markdown table (not a JSX Table component).

Note: The lastUpdated date may already be 2026-02-28 — check before changing.

userStories.title: Fix ProgressFlow illustration IPC mechanism label
userStories.description: As a platform contributor, I want the ProgressFlow illustration to correctly show 'CustomEvent' instead of 'window.postMessage' between the Adapter IIFE and Content Script steps so that the diagram matches the actual implementation.
acceptanceCriteria: The text label between Step 2 (Adapter IIFE) and Step 3 (Content Script) in the ProgressFlow illustration reads 'CustomEvent' (not 'window.postMessage')
cd docs && npm run build passes
cd docs && npm run type-check passes
cd docs && npm run lint passes
cd docs && npm run knip passes
cd docs && npm run format:check passes
userStories.notes:
File: docs/components/illustrations.tsx

Line 1488: The text element reads 'window.postMessage'. Change to 'CustomEvent'.

The actual code at platform/browser-extension/src/tool-dispatch.ts uses CustomEvent on the shared document object — the MAIN world fires a CustomEvent on document, and the ISOLATED world content script picks it up via document.addEventListener(). window.postMessage is never used in this flow.

The SVG text element is at:
```jsx
<text x="282" y="170" fontSize="9" fontFamily="var(--font-mono), monospace" fill="var(--color-foreground)" opacity="0.5">
  window.postMessage
</text>
```

Change the text content to 'CustomEvent'. You may need to adjust the x position slightly to keep it visually centered, since 'CustomEvent' is shorter than 'window.postMessage'.
